name: Banana-Date-Oatmeal Cookies
recipeIngredient: 2 large bananas (at least two cups mashed)
1 Cup cake flour (all-purpose is okay but not as tender)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Line two baking sheets with parchment paper or coat with non-stick spray.
3. Toasting oats: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add half the oats. Toast, stirring frequently until the oats begin to color and become fragrant (about 5 minutes).
4. Mash the bananas and mix with the wet ingredients and brown sugar. If the banana isn't overripe, microwave it for about 30 seconds to make it mushy.
5. Toast the grains one cup at a time and add them to the wet mixture as the next batch is toasting (this allows the grains to absorb some moisture).
6. Stir in the remaining ingredients.
7. Pour some whole wheat flour into a dish. Scoop up about a tablespoonful of the dough and dip one side into the whole wheat flour. Press the floured side onto the baking sheet (this will prevent sticking during baking and storage).
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until lightly browned. Transfer cookies to a wire rack to cool. Makes 3 dozen cookies.
9. Store in an airtight container.
